comment.body:
Seems like the more necessary the good people have to buy is, the more a retailer can get away with hiking its cost. However, the less necessary a good is, the more likely a retailer will eat the tariff cost.

So ya just seems people will adjust their consumption behavior accordingly, and businesses will have to eat it if they are on the fringe.

Probably not great for restaurants I would imagine. A lot of people could save a lot a money by cutting down their restaurant consumption.
